title: Siege of Otoh Gunga
content:
{{Top|canon=Attack on Otoh Gunga}} {{Otheruses|Battle of Naboo}} {{Conflicting|''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''|"[[End Game]]"}} {{Battle |prev=*[[Duel on Tatooine/Legends|Duel on Tatooine]]{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}} *[[Search for Captain Kael]] |conc= |next=[[Battle of the Sacred Place]]''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' |image=[[File:Naboo_Swamp_Battle.jpg]] |name=Siege of Otoh Gunga |conflict=[[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]{{Film|I}} |date=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] |place=[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] |result=*[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] victory *Gungan evacuation of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] |side1=*[[Gungan society]] **[[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] |side2=*Trade Federation **[[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] *[[Order of the Sith Lords]] |side3=[[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] |side4= |commanders1= *[[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] *Boss [[Ganne]]† *A [[Unidentified Gungan general|Gungan general]]† *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]]{{FactFile|137|CAP 1-2|Captain Tarpals}} |commanders2=*[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] *[[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] *[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] *[[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] |commanders3= [[Maris Magneta]]† |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Militiagung/Legends|Militiagungs]] *[[Kaadu/Legends|Kaadu]] cavalry *[[Catapult/Legends|Catapults]] mounted on [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaas]] |forces2=*Half a company of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] *A contingent of [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] *[[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platforms]] *[[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]] *1 [[S-DST]] |forces3=*Maris Magneta *Magneta's bodyguard *Several [[Farmer/Legends|fieldworkers]] |forces4= |casual1=*Boss Ganne *Ankura general *Militiagungs and Gungan citizens |casual2=Many STAPs destroyed |casual3=All participants [[Death/Legends|killed]] |casual4= |civilian=Several Naboo traders captured }} {{Quote|My troops are in position to begin searching the swamps for these rumored underwater villages. They will not stay hidden for long.|OOM-9, to Nute Gunray|{{Film|I}}}} The '''Siege of Otoh Gunga''' was a raid attempted by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] on the [[Water/Legends|underwater]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[City/Legends|cities]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. ==Prelude== ===The Invasion of Naboo=== {{Quote|Once those droids take control of the surface, they will take control of you.''"
"''Mesa no tink so. Day not know of uss-en.''"
"''You and the Naboo form a symbiont circle. What happens to one of you will affect the other. You must understand this.|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Boss Rugor Nass|}} [[File:OOM9Reporting-TPM.png|thumb|right|300px|OOM-9 reports to Nute Gunray that he is ready to begin the search for the Gungan villages.]] In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation of Planets]] launched a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in protest of [[Senate Resolution BR-0371]] on the orders of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], the secret identity of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] Palpatine of Naboo.[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] Following a [[Mission to Naboo|failed attempt]] by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to resolve the blockade, the Trade Federation escalated its blockade into a full-scale [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of Naboo, initially targeting the surface-dwelling [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]] population rather than the indigenous [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who lived [[Water/Legends|underwater]]. Jinn and Kenobi met with the Gungan leader [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] in the Gungan [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] and tried to warn him that the Trade Federation would target the Gungans after conquering the Humans, but Nass believed that the Trade Federation would ignore the Gungans. However, many of the Gungans were growing nervous as the invasion continued, and so Nass made plans for a possible evacuation of the Gungan cities.{{FactFile|8|NAS 1-4|Boss Nass}} Rumors of Gungan sightings reached [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] soon after their [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] landed on Naboo. The company initially ignored the rumors but felt that they could not continue doing so after they [[Capture of Theed/Legends|captured]] the Human capital [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. A [[Unidentified mariner|mariner]] from the [[Capture of Harte Secur|captured]] city [[Harte Secur]] told the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] about the Gungan bubble city [[Rellias]] and its leader, Boss [[Ganne]]. OOM-9 organized for the Droid Army to search the [[Gungan Swamp|swamps]] for the Gungans, and reported directly to Gunray in [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]] when they were ready to begin the search. Gunray believed that the Gungans would be crushed shortly, and regarded the planet as already under his control.[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]] ===Sith intervention=== {{Dialogue|Gunray|The Gungans need not concern us.|Maul|Why is that, Viceroy?|Gunray|The amphibians would not risk engaging our overwhelming force.|Maul|The Gungans have a standing army thousands strong and strategic plasma weaponry.|Haako|We didn't know.|Maul|Now you do.|attr=Darth Maul updates Nute Gunray and Rune Haako on the need to flush out the Gungans|src=}} [[File:Maul_Neimoidians.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Maul (left) was sent to work with the Neimoidians to find the Gungans.]] OOM-9's initial searches did not lead to any Gungan villages or to the capture of any Gungans. Sometime after a Trade Federation [[plasma mining camp]] was [[Search for Captain Kael|destroyed]], the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] was ordered by Sidious to go to Naboo to assist the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidians]] of the Trade Federation root out the remaining resistance, including the Gungans. Maul had failed in his [[Duel on Tatooine/Legends|attempt]] to kidnap [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] of Naboo on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and while Maul was away Sidious worked on modifying his [[Grand Plan/Legends|plans]]. When Maul met with Gunray and his advisor, [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]], he inquired about the Gungans. To Maul's disgust, Gunray and Haako attempted to minimize the truth that they had not located any of the Gungan settlements. Maul caught Gunray lying that they had discarded thousands of atomized bodies of Gungans into the sea. Gunray attempted to deflect by stating that the Gungans were not a threat to their invasion, but Maul informed him that they had their own [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]] and sufficient [[Plasma/Legends|plasma]] [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] to be a threat. The Neimoidians then informed Maul that OOM-9 was in charge of the search for the Gungans, and Maul ordered Gunray to inform the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] that he would assume command of the search. Maul departed on his [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]], [[Bloodfin/Legends|''Bloodfin'']] to OOM-9's forward observation post next to an [[Insect/Legends|insect]]-filled [[Swamp/Legends|marsh]]. ==The siege== ===Locating the cities=== ====Maul's search==== {{Quote|This isn't the time for subtlety. Poison the waters. If that doesn't bring the Gungans to the surface, then drain the marsh.|Darth Maul, ordering OOM-9|}} [[File:Maul_on_Patrol.png|thumb|right|300px|Darth Maul searches for the Gungans.]] By the time Maul arrived, OOM-9's [[Droid/Legends|droids]] had shot and [[Death/Legends|killed]] four Gungans who had been caught exchanging goods with traders outside the city [[Moenia/Legends|Moenia]], while the traders were interred in [[Camp Six]]. When the Sith apprentice arrived, he asked OOM-9 how many Gungans had been captured, to which the battle droid lied that forty-seven had been captured after being ordered to say so by computers aboard the [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ship]], the [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Paala]]. Maul decided not to call out OOM-9 on the lie and instead had the droid take him to see the four Gungan corpses, at which point he made further inquires about the search. OOM-9 revealed that they were yet to find any trace of Rellias and speculated that the Gungans were [[Jammer/Legends|jamming]] their scanners. Maul was paranoid that the Gungans would interfere with his personal objective to capture Amidala and kill Jinn and Kenobi, who were now serving as her protectors. Therefore, he ordered OOM-9 to [[Poison/Legends|poison]] the nearby waters and drain the whole marsh if that failed to expose any Gungans. Maul rode his speeder to [[Summit Farm Blossom Winery]] to find [[Maris Magneta]], the former head of security for Naboo's previous [[King/Legends|king]], the deceased [[Ars Veruna/Legends|Ars Veruna]]. Sidious had told Maul that she would potentially be able to help locate the Gungan cities. Magneta invited Maul into her home and offered him a beverage, only to acknowledge that she did not know how to find the cities. She offered him the name [[Leika]], a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] surveyor for an offworld mining company that had been contracted by Veruna to analyse Gungan territory. Leika had been trapped on Naboo when the Trade Federation's blockade started, so Magneta suggested that Maul could find the Bothan at a Trade Federation detention camp. ====Ambush and Bothan data==== {{Quote|The name of the governor of Rellias?''"
"''Boss, Boss Ganne. An Ankura Gungan—the ones with green skin and hooded eyes.|Maul receives information from Leika|}} [[File:Otoh_Gunga.png|thumb|left|300px|Maul received information from Leika on how to enter the hydrostatic bubbles of Otoh Gunga (pictured).]] Just as Maul was about to leave, Magneta acknowledged her involvement in the past schemes of the Neimoidians, Palpatine, and [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Hego Damask]]—Palpatine's [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], Darth Plagueis. Maul had been previously unaware of Damask, whom Magneta claimed had been the one controlling Palpatine. The apprentice knew that he could not allow any links between Palpatine and the Neimoidians to be uncovered, and realized he was being ambushed as he heard Magneta reach for a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] hidden beneath her apron and several armed [[Farmer/Legends|fieldworkers]] approaching the building. The Sith reached out and broke Magneta's neck and kicked her bodyguard as he entered the room. The fieldworkers fired blasters at the door, but Maul dove out the window in a somersault, landed in the middle of them, and killed them one by one. The Sith apprentice travelled to Camp Six and located Leika, who feared that Maul had come to kill him. The Bothan was relieved to learn otherwise, and agreed to help Maul in the hope that he could arrange for passage off Naboo. Leika inserted a [[Data crystal/Legends|data crystal]] into a reader that revealed a 3D map of Naboo's swamps, and at Maul's request gave him the coordinates of Rellias. Leika also described how the Trade Federation could enter the Gungan's [[Hydrostatic bubble/Legends|hydrostatic bubbles]] and gave information about a fortified plasma channel that led to Otoh Gunga and other major Gungan cities and their [[Gungan Sacred Place/Legends|Sacred Place]]. Maul took the data crystal and reader from Leika and acknowledged that their terms would be honoured. ===Fall of Rellias=== ====First blows==== {{Quote|The location coordinates for Rellias can be accessed from the menu. Transmit the data to your STAP patrols and order them to saturate the location with depth charges. Then prepare the S-DST for immediate embarkation and meet me on board.|Darth Maul, to OOM-9|}} [[File:STAP_TPM.png|thumb|right|250px|Many Single Trooper Aerial Platforms (one pictured) were destroyed by the Gungans.]] OOM-9's effort to poison the marsh had darkened the waters and killed many [[Gooberfish/Legends|gooberfish]], but when the droids attempted to drain it they discovered that the marsh waters replenished themselves due to a connection between vast systems of underground water. When Maul returned, OOM-9 reported the conditions, after which Maul handed the data crystal to the droid and ordered him to transmit the data to their [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platform]] patrols. Maul instructed the STAPS to cover Rellias's location with depth charges. Maul and OOM-9 travelled to Rellias aboard an [[S-DST]], an aquatic destroyer, along with half a company of B1 battle droids, a full contingent of [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]]. By mid-afternoon, the S-DST had maneuvered through a series of channels to an enormous lake. The STAPS continued detonating their depth charges on the water as Maul prepared himself for combat. As the STAPs drew close to Rellias, they were decimated by [[Otolla/Legends|Otolla]] Gungan [[Militiagung/Legends|militiagungs]] wielding plasma [[Booma/Legends|boomas]] and [[Energy lance/Legends|energy lances]] while standing on nearby stone fortifications. Then, a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Gungan [[Submarine/Legends|submersibles]] rose out of the water and began firing on the S-DST. The droids landed their destroyer on the beach so that they could disembark, where they were met with a [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]]-mount cavalry charge, led by Ganne and an [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Unidentified Gungan general|general]]. Behind the cavalry, the droids were assaulted by [[Catapult/Legends|catapults]] strapped to the backs of [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaas]]. The battle droids marched directly towards the charging Gungans, firing at them with their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. ====Capture of Ganne==== {{Quote|The route to Otoh Gunga.''"
"''Yousa needen to be knowen desa ways to Otoh Gunga,''"
"''Tell me,''"
"''Mesa tell yousa. Yousa take yous mackineeks through da Rellias Straits.''"
"''You’ll open the gates when we arrive.''"
"''Mesa open dissa gates when wesa riven.|Darth Maul mind tricks Ganne into taking the Trade Federation to Otoh Gunga|}} [[File:Militiagung-SWCTP.png|thumb|left|200px|The Gungans charged with kaadu cavalry (one pictured) against the Trade Federation.]] Maul jumped off the S-DST and leapt into the air to hang from the footrest of one of the few remaining STAPs. Several Gungans fired at Maul, who moved around to evade their aim and blocked some shots with his [[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. As he came within twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] from Ganne, the Sith apprentice used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to launched himself through a crowd of mounted Gungans, cutting through them efficiently. Maul recognized that the Gungans were surprised at his presence, as a Sith had not wielded a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] in open battle in nearly one thousand years. Gungan body parts were strewn in all directions as Maul drew closer to Ganne and cut the legs out from some of the kaadu. At five meters from Ganne, Maul [[Force jump/Legends|leapt]] into the air, decapitating the general and knocking Ganne from his kaadu with his left hand. Ganne scrambled to grab an [[Electropole/Legends|electropole]], but Maul disarmed him and dragged him to the [[Tree/Legends|treeline]] on the edge of the battlefield. Maul identified Ganne as weak-willed and elected not to use force, deactivating his lightsaber and instead using a [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] to overpower Ganne's will and get him to lead the way to Otoh Gunga. Ganne told Maul that they would need to pass through the [[Rellias Straits]] to reach the capital, and agreed to open the [[Gates of Rellias]] on the route. Maul then dragged Ganne to the S-DST. ====Otoh Gunga evacuated==== {{Quote|We gather our most precious belongings. It is not easy to leave our homes. There are many things we leave behind. But our lives are more important than our homes.|Boss Rugor Nass, on the evacuation of Otoh Gunga|}} [[File:Gungan_evacuation.png|thumb|right|250px|Gungans prepare to leave Otoh Gunga with their belongings.]] A Gungan [[Scout/Legends|scout]] witnessed a group of STAPs and B1 battle droids and reported it to Otoh Gunga,''[[Gungan Trouble!]]'' and word of the fall of Rellias quickly reached the capital. Boss Nass met with the [[Gungan High Council/Legends|Gungan High Council]] and warned them that they needed to evacuate before the Trade Federation arrived. Many of the council members wanted to remain, but [[Representative]] [[Been]] convinced them to leave for shelter at the Sacred Place. The Gungans of Otoh Gunga found it difficult to pack their belongings but knew their lives were at risk if they remained. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]] supervised the evacuation of thousands of Gungans from Otoh Gunga, and almost all of the one million inhabitants of the city were able to escape due to the massive size of the Gungan Swamp.{{FactFile|65|NAB 25-28|Naboo – Swamp}} Nass led the Gungans to the Sacred Place, where they prayed to their deities, the [[Gud/Legends|guds]], for protection. ====The mind tricked boss==== {{Quote|Open the gate, Gungan.''"
"''No! Mesa no betrayin' Otoh Gunga!''"
"''You will open the gate.''"
[hypnotized] "''Mesa… open gate.|Darth Maul and Ganne at the Gates of Rellias|audio=MaulControlsGanne.flac|}} [[File:Rellias_channel.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Trade Federation Droid Army travelled through the Rellias Straits (entrance pictured).]] When the droids reached the Gates of Rellias, Ganne attempted to resist Maul's command to open them to avoid betraying Otoh Gunga. Maul reiterated his command and the boss obeyed, opening the gates for them. The droids travelled on the destroyer through the straits to [[Lake Paonga/Legends|Lake Paonga]], the site of Otoh Gunga, and groups of militiagungs on the shore fired at the droids with their boomas. Maul ceased the attacks by securing Ganne to the S-DST's bow as a figurehead; the Gungans were unwilling to attack the boss and instead brandished their electropoles and made war cries. With their STAPs destroyed, OOM-9 ordered his battle droids to set off explosives in the lake, and Maul realized that the city must have been evacuated as no bodies emerged from the turmoiled lake surface. That fact was confirmed when OOM-9's drone submersibles returned from scouting missions. Maul hauled Ganne onto the deck to find out where Nass would be hiding, but the boss was able to resist Maul's mind control powers. Maul drew out his lightsaber and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] Ganne, who slowly revealed that Nass had gone to the Sacred Place, which Maul felt showed a degree of honor. Once Ganne was dead, Maul rolled his blistered body into the lake, at which point OOM-9 informed him that he had received a communication from Gunray that Queen Amidala and the Jedi had returned to Naboo. Maul returned to Theed to discuss strategy with the Neimoidians. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Now dat Otoh Gunga issa no more, wesa must unite all deh Gungans at deh Sacred Place.|Boss Nass|}} [[File:Nass_on_Sacred_Place.png|thumb|right|300px|Boss Nass formed an alliance with the Naboo at the Sacred Place in the aftermath of the siege.]] The Gungans at the Sacred Place grew anxious in the days following the siege, and as supplies dwindled fights broke out. There was division over whether they should return to Otoh Gunga or to retaliate against the Trade Federation. Captain Tarpals fought several skirmishes against droid patrols to keep the Sacred Place hidden,` and he and General [[Tobler Ceel/Legends|Tobler Ceel]] began planning a guerilla warfare campaign that involved hit and run strikes on the Trade Federation followed by swift retreats into the swamps. However, Nass urged his people to be patient. Queen Amidala had returned to Naboo with the intention of fighting against the Trade Federation, and planned an alliance with the Gungans to do so. She hoped to use her Gungan companion [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] to meet with Boss Nass, but Binks found Otoh Gunga deserted and many of its buildings scorched with blaster-fire.[[The Phantom Menace (Mighty Chronicles)|''The Phantom Menace'' Mighty Chronicles adaptation]] The Gungan returned to the surface to report his findings, leading Kenobi and Amidala's [[Head of Security/Legends|Head of Security]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]] to believe that the Gungans had been killed or interned. Binks accurately guessed that they had instead evacuated to the Sacred Place and lead the Queen's entourage there, risking his own life due to his severe unpopularity amongst the Gungans.{{FactFile|59|NAB 23-24|Naboo – Gungan Sacred Place}} Tarpals and several Gungan guards met the queen as the group neared the Sacred Place, and Amidala initially feared that they blamed her for the invasion as much as the Trade Federation.''[[Star Wars Journal: Queen Amidala]]'' Tarpals took the group to meet with Nass, where Amidala begged for Nass's help in retaking Naboo. Nass was impressed by Amidala's show of humility and accepted, overcoming centuries of distrust between Humans and Gungans. Together, they created a plan where the Gungan Grand Army would divert the bulk of the Trade Federation Droid Army on the [[Great Grass Plains/Legends|Great Grass Plains]] near Theed while Amidala infiltrated Theed Palace to capture Gunray and [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] from the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]] would eliminate the orbiting droid control ship to deactivate the Trade Federation's army. Maul spied on some of the planning process at the Sacred Place, having used the information gained from torturing Boss Ganne to locate it. ==Behind the scenes== ===Origins and appearances=== The Siege of Otoh Gunga was created for the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], written and directed by [[George Lucas]]. The siege did not take place in the revised rough draft of the film, instead the Trade Federation is described as not having discovered Otoh Gunga. Amidala and her entourage meet with Nass inside the city and persuade him to join them on account of having no other allies to call on. The siege was added to the story by the time the final draft was written, and a brief scene was included where Binks enters Otoh Gunga after the siege and sees signs of a battle such as blaster-marked buildings.[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']] The first published mention of the siege was in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|novelization]] of ''The Phantom Menace'', published on [[April 21]], [[1999]], prior to the film's premiere on [[May 19]], 1999.''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' The first narrative source to depict the evacuation of Otoh Gunga was the [[2000]] picture book ''[[Gungan Trouble!]]'', written by [[Liza Baker]] and illustrated by [[Jerry Vanderstelt]]. The [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' was the first source to depict the violence between the Trade Federation and the Gungans, rather than just its aftermath. The game depicted the siege in the fifth and sixth levels of the Trade Federation campaign, which were titled "The Search for an Underwater Kingdom" and "The Siege of Otoh Gunga," the latter of which named the event. The former level depicts the breach of the Rellias Straits, while the latter involves the attack on Otoh Gunga. ===''Galactic Battlegrounds'' details=== ===="The Search for an Underwater Kingdom"==== [[File:Otoh_Gunga_landing_force.png|thumb|right|300px|Darth Maul and the Droid Army at the initial staging ground]] The short story "[[End Game]]," written by [[James Luceno]] and published in the [[2012]] rerelease of ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, portrayed the initial stage of the siege as taking place on a river bank leading into Lake Paonga, contrary to the portrayal in "The Search for an Underwater Kingdom" from ''Galactic Battlegrounds'', in which the abduction of Boss Ganne takes place entirely on land. This article follows the short stories version of events and the details from ''Galactic Battlegrounds'' and the Prima guide that do not contradict it. The remaining details are included below. Darth Maul is present at the beginning of the level with the stealth ship [[Scimitar/Legends|''Scimitar'']], rather than the ''Bloodfin'' as described in "End Game." Also present are six [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|beetle droids]], four [[Heavy Battle Droid/Legends|Heavy Battle Droids]], two droidekas, two [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]], and two [[Energy pummel/Legends|energy pummels]]. The strategy guide recommends that the player send Maul ahead of the rest of their units on account of his ability to defeat any unit in the level. The guide recommends that the player take an obscured path through a forest to find hidden [[Gungan farms]], where Maul can destroy four kaadu troopers, the worker [[Glurrg]]s who are attending the farms, and the farms themselves.[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] Destroying the farms and the Gungans there is not required to complete the level and can be skipped. Afterwards, the guide suggests that the player send the other droids through the farms and onto [[Ganne's compound]], where they will encounter Gungan forces including troopers, kaadu troopers, catapaults, and [[Booma Trooper]]s. After defeating them, the player can proceed east to the compound's command center, and notes that Maul could destroy it by himself if the player is patient, but that they should instead send their pummels in. Once the building is destroyed, the player can access a short path to find Ganne on a kaadu. [[File:Maul_Ganne.png|thumb|left|300px|Darth Maul approaches Boss Ganne.]] Once the player sends Maul near Ganne, the Gungan joins the player's side under Maul's mind control. The dialogue spoken by both characters differs from that in "End Game," with Ganne initially trying to resist Maul's domination. Ganne directs the Trade Federation to go southwest to the Rellias Channel. On the way, the player encounters four Heavy Battle Droids and two droidekas—reported as four in the strategy guide—that join them as reinforcements. As the player continues south, they encounter more Gungans, the strategy guide states that the player can overpower them and that they should use Maul to mind trick some of them to the player's side. The player eventually reaches the Gates of Rellias, and the guide notes that they could be destroyed with the player's pummels. Instead, dialogue is triggered in which Ganne attempts to resist betraying his people by opening the gates, but is compelled by Maul to do so. The final area of the level is guarded by two turrets, and Gungan forces, including heavy militiagungs and fambaas and [[Lerraa]]s mounted with weapons. The guide recommends that the player use the droidekas to kill the Gungans while the pummels eliminate the turrets. Once the player has done so, they can send their forces through the final gate and come to the entrance of Lake Paonga. The level ends with the voice of Darth Sidious celebrating the Trade Federation's success. The guide notes that the only major way the player can lose the level is if Ganne is killed. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Siege of Otoh Gunga}} *''[[Star Wars: Galactic
